behaviors.description: The traveling-salesman heuristic produces a route that is no more than 1.5x the length of the optimal route for inputs up to 20 stops.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional choice: Unit, because this behavior is algorithm-quality at a single heuristic boundary (route length ratio vs optimal) and can be exercised with in-memory coordinates/distance matrices. PASS2-AUDIT: Integration rejected because behavior text is only about heuristic output quality, not module handoff; System rejected because no real OS/network/subprocess is required; Agentic/Workflow rejected because no AI/ML step exists. REJECTION-GATE evidence: behavior text says only "heuristic produces a route ... up to 20 stops" and does not require geocoding/API/orchestration.
behaviors.test_description: Call the heuristic function directly on generated and fixed stop sets (n<=20), compute exact optimal tour for small/medium cases in-process, and assert heuristic_length/optimal_length <= 1.5. This catches real algorithm regressions without any external dependencies.

behaviors.description: The delivery time window estimates are computed correctly from the distance between consecutive stops and the configured average speed for the road type.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional choice: Unit, since this is deterministic computation (distance + speed config -> time window). PASS2-AUDIT: Integration considered for config/module wiring, but rejected because behavior text focuses on correctness of the computation itself, not cross-module state propagation; System rejected (no real infra needed); Agentic/Workflow rejected (no model output). REJECTION-GATE evidence: behavior specifies formula inputs/outputs, not infrastructure or orchestration failure boundaries.
behaviors.test_description: Test the estimator function with table-driven cases across road types, distances, and speeds (including rounding/edge cases), asserting exact expected window outputs. Keep dependencies in-process (config fixture/map) to detect arithmetic and lookup logic defects.

behaviors.description: The shift-hours validator rejects a route whose total estimated time exceeds the driver's remaining shift hours, returning the excess amount.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional choice: Unit, because this is validator logic on two values (estimated total vs remaining shift) plus excess calculation. PASS2-AUDIT: Integration rejected since no module interaction is required by the behavior text; System rejected (no OS/network/subprocess); Agentic/Workflow rejected (no AI). REJECTION-GATE evidence: behavior is strictly local validation: "rejects ... returning the excess amount."
behaviors.test_description: Invoke validator with boundary cases (equal, just under, just over, large overage) and assert rejection flag plus exact excess duration. This directly catches comparison/units/math bugs at the true failure boundary.

behaviors.description: When one address in the list fails geocoding (API returns no results), the service skips that stop and optimizes the remaining addresses, reporting which addresses were skipped.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional choice: Integration, because the failure appears at module handoff: geocoding result handling -> filtering -> optimizer input -> skipped-address reporting. PASS2-AUDIT (INTEGRATION-BRAKE): (a) Real infra required? No; a stub geocoding adapter returning "no results" reproduces the failure. (b) Real AI/ML output? No. (c) Multiple agentic steps? No. (d) LLM-governed handoff? No. System rejected since real HTTP/network is not necessary for this behavior; Unit rejected because single-function tests can miss orchestration defects across these modules. REJECTION-GATE evidence: behavior explicitly says "service skips ... optimizes remaining ... reporting skipped," which is cross-module orchestration.
behaviors.test_description: Wire service + geocoder adapter stub + optimizer module in-process. Return no-results for one specific address and valid coordinates for others. Assert: failed address excluded from optimizer input, route includes only geocoded stops, and response contains exact skipped-address list.

behaviors.description: Duplicate addresses in the input list are consolidated into a single stop with combined delivery items, not visited twice.
behaviors.justification: PASS1 provisional choice: Integration, because correctness spans dedup/merge logic and downstream route construction (ensuring not visited twice). PASS2-AUDIT (INTEGRATION-BRAKE): (a) No real OS/network/subprocess required. (b) No AI/ML dependence. (c) No multi-agentic chain. (d) No LLM orchestration. Unit considered, but rejected: a unit test of dedup alone can pass while optimizer still receives/uses duplicates. System rejected due lack of real infra dependency. REJECTION-GATE evidence: behavior requires both "consolidated ... combined delivery items" and "not visited twice," implying cross-module handoff into routing.
behaviors.test_description: Run in-process service pipeline with duplicate-address inputs carrying different items. Assert output has one stop for that address, merged item set/count, and final route visits that stop exactly once.
